surface.name: Your First Render Deploy, server-side Web Service using the Dashboard path
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): when official docs present peer routes, commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart, dashboard/console over CLI when both are peers, and the route with an explicit vendor time claim or fewest documented steps when tied. Committed to already-reconstructed surface `Your First Render Deploy, server-side Web Service using the Dashboard path`. Prior basis: Render's documentation home promotes 'Ship your first app' and links 'Deploy my code' to the official Your First Render Deploy tutorial. Render's service-type guide calls a web service the most common first service. The tutorial, however, presents Dashboard, CLI, and coding-agent deployment as user-selected peers. This record reconstructs the Dashboard route because it is the first documented tab, but does not claim Render recommends it over the other two routes.

alternatives_considered.name: Render CLI deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The first-deploy tutorial presents CLI as a peer selected by developer preference and does not rank it above or below Dashboard.

alternatives_considered.name: Coding-agent deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The first-deploy tutorial presents a coding agent as a peer selected by developer preference and does not declare it the default.

alternatives_considered.name: Static Site deployment
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The selected developer intent is a server-side application. Render directs applications containing server-side code to Web Service instead.

alternatives_considered.name: Language or framework quickstart
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The selected intent is to deploy an existing app. The docs offer quickstarts as the example-app route.

entry_point.developer_intent: Deploy an existing server-side web application from a GitHub repository and verify it is publicly reachable on Render.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has arrived after discovery and intends to deploy existing code.
The application source already exists in a GitHub repository the developer can access.
The application contains server-side code, so Render's documented service-type rule directs it to Web Service.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Congratulations! You've deployed your first app on Render.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: The developer's first server-side web app has a Live Render deploy and serves its root content from the assigned onrender.com URL.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The deploy status is Live, and opening the service's onrender.com URL in a browser serves the app's root-path content.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The final numbered section, 'Open your app,' ends with 'Congratulations! You've deployed your first app on Render,' immediately before 'Next steps.'

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: The official first-deploy tutorial itself names the milestone after the developer opens the deployed app, then moves to a separate Next steps section. This record does not substitute signup, service creation, or Live status alone for that documented terminal state.

prerequisites.requirement: A GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket account with a repository containing the web app; this reconstructed route uses GitHub.
Permission to authorize Render to access the selected GitHub repositories.
A server-side web app that listens for HTTP requests and binds its public HTTP server to host 0.0.0.0, preferably using Render's PORT environment variable.
The application-specific build command and start command.
Any environment variables or secrets required for the app to build and run.
A payment method is not required for the tutorial's Free-resource path.

primary_path.action: Open the Render documentation home and follow Deploy my code under Ship your first app.
details: This opens the official Your First Render Deploy tutorial.
primary_path.input: Intent to deploy an existing web app.
primary_path.output: The first-deploy tutorial.
primary_path.success_signal: The page heading is Your First Render Deploy.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Select the Dashboard deployment method for this reconstructed route.
details: The tutorial also offers CLI and Coding Agent tabs and tells the developer to select the preferred method.
primary_path.output: Dashboard route selected.
primary_path.success_signal: Dashboard instructions are visible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Render does not document one preferred interface, which prevents this record from claiming a canonical primary route.

primary_path.action: Follow Sign up for Render and create a free Render account.
details: Render documents Google, GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and email/password as supported login methods.
primary_path.input: A supported login identity and the undisclosed registration-form inputs.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Render account with Dashboard access.
primary_path.success_signal: The developer can open Account Settings in the Dashboard.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The public docs do not enumerate registration fields or any email-verification transition; the registration page is a client-rendered application shell.

primary_path.action: Open Account Settings, scroll to Account Security, and locate Git Deployment Credentials.
primary_path.output: Git Deployment Credentials controls.
primary_path.success_signal: Add credential is available.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: None documented.

primary_path.action: Click Add credential and choose GitHub.
details: GitLab and Bitbucket are also documented provider choices.
primary_path.output: Redirect to GitHub authorization.
primary_path.success_signal: GitHub displays Render's authorization flow.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Provider authorization is required before private or owned repositories can be selected.

primary_path.action: Authorize Render to access the needed GitHub repositories.
details: Repository access must include the app repository.
primary_path.output: A linked GitHub deployment credential.
primary_path.success_signal: GitHub redirects back to Render and the Dashboard displays accessible repositories.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Insufficient GitHub App repository access prevents the repository from appearing.

primary_path.action: Classify the server-side application as a Web Service.
details: Render says Web Service is for apps with server-side code and calls it the most common first service.
primary_path.input: Knowledge that the app runs server-side code and listens for HTTP requests.
primary_path.output: Web Service selected as the service type.
primary_path.success_signal: The chosen service type matches Render's documented rule for the app.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A static-only app follows the Static Site branch instead.

primary_path.action: In the Dashboard, click New and select Web Service.
primary_path.input: Authenticated account and selected service type.
primary_path.output: The Web Service creation form and source selector.
primary_path.success_signal: Available repositories and source options are displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If the Git provider was not connected earlier, the form prompts for that connection.

primary_path.action: Select Git Provider as the source type.
details: Public Git Repository and Existing Image are alternative source paths.
primary_path.output: Repository list for the linked provider.
primary_path.success_signal: The app repository is present in the list.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing repository can indicate insufficient GitHub App repository access.

primary_path.action: Select the repository containing the web app and click Connect.
primary_path.input: A GitHub repository accessible through the linked credential.
primary_path.output: The remainder of the Web Service creation form.
primary_path.success_signal: Service configuration fields are displayed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The repository cannot be selected without provider access.

primary_path.action: Provide the service Name.
details: The name identifies the service in the Dashboard and contributes to its onrender.com subdomain.
primary_path.output: Named Web Service configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The Name field is accepted.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Exact naming validation is not specified in the inspected docs.

primary_path.action: Choose the Region where the service will run.
primary_path.output: Region assigned to the service configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: A region is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The general tutorial does not prescribe one region.

primary_path.action: Choose the repository Branch to build.
details: For a monorepo, also specify the Root Directory containing the application.
primary_path.input: The deployment branch and, conditionally, application root directory.
primary_path.output: Source revision and root configured.
primary_path.success_signal: The intended branch is selected.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect root directory can prevent build files from being found.

primary_path.action: Select the application's Language runtime.
details: Render also supports Docker for an app built from a Dockerfile.
primary_path.input: The app's language or Docker runtime.
primary_path.output: Runtime selected.
primary_path.success_signal: The selected runtime matches the repository.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An incorrect runtime can cause build or start failure.

primary_path.action: Provide the application Build Command.
details: Render uses this command to install dependencies and build the service from source.
primary_path.output: Build command stored in the service configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The form accepts the command.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or invalid build command can cause deployment failure.

primary_path.action: Provide the application Start Command.
details: Render uses this command to start the built service.
primary_path.output: Start command stored in the service configuration.
primary_path.success_signal: The form accepts the command.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A missing or invalid start command can cause deployment failure.

primary_path.action: Choose the Free instance type for the tutorial path.
details: The instance type determines RAM, CPU, and cost; the tutorial instructs choosing Free for a free deployment.
primary_path.output: Free compute plan selected.
primary_path.success_signal: Free is shown as the chosen instance type.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Free web services have documented operational limitations, but no payment is required for this first-deploy route.

primary_path.action: Add any environment variables or secrets the application requires.
details: The tutorial says omitted variables can be added later with a redeploy.
primary_path.output: Required build-time and runtime values configured.
primary_path.success_signal: All values needed for this app's first build and start are present.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: This step is conditional; missing required values can cause build or runtime failure.

primary_path.action: Submit the service-creation form.
details: The first-deploy tutorial labels the control Deploy; the Web Services reference says Create Web Service.
primary_path.output: A new service and an initiated first build and deploy.
primary_path.success_signal: The service page exposes deploy progress.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The differing official control labels are recorded as an uncertainty.

primary_path.action: Create the Web Service and start its first build and deploy.
details: Render exposes deploy progress on the service page and Events page.
primary_path.input: Submitted service configuration and repository source.
primary_path.output: An in-progress deploy with logs.
primary_path.success_signal: Build and start log lines begin to appear.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Deployment duration is not numerically documented; the developer waits for completion or failure.

primary_path.action: Open the new service's page and monitor the deploy logs.
details: Follow the build and start commands in the log explorer.
primary_path.output: Visible deploy status and logs.
primary_path.success_signal: The deploy status changes to Live.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: If status changes to Failed, review logs, correct the issue, and push a new commit to trigger another deploy.

primary_path.action: Run the configured build command and start command.
details: For a Web Service, Render must detect the app listening on a public HTTP port.
primary_path.input: Repository contents, runtime, commands, and environment variables.
primary_path.output: A running service instance if build and start succeed.
primary_path.success_signal: Build and start complete, and the service binds to the public HTTP port.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Build, start, missing configuration, or port-binding errors result in a failed deploy and appear in logs.

primary_path.action: Mark the successful deploy Live and expose the service's unique onrender.com URL.
primary_path.input: Successful build, start, and public port binding.
primary_path.output: Live deploy and public service URL.
primary_path.success_signal: Live status and an onrender.com URL are visible on the service page.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A failed deploy is marked Failed instead of Live.

primary_path.action: Click the service's onrender.com URL.
details: The tutorial states that the service serves content for its root path.
primary_path.input: The URL shown on the service page.
primary_path.output: A browser request to the deployed app's root path.
primary_path.success_signal: The browser opens the service URL.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: A Free service that has later spun down can require a documented spin-up wait, but the tutorial does not place that later idle state in the initial deploy path.

primary_path.action: Serve the deployed application's root-path content at the onrender.com URL.
primary_path.output: Application content from the deployed Web Service.
primary_path.success_signal: The app's root content is visible in the browser, after which the tutorial declares the first app deployed.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: An application-level error can still prevent useful content from rendering even if a deploy reached Live; the tutorial's verification step is to open the app.

branches.path: Use the tutorial's CLI tab: install the Render CLI, authenticate, select a workspace, and run render services create with service-specific options.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The route has additional installation and CLI-authentication gates but targets the same deployed-app terminal state.

branches.condition: The developer prefers a supported coding agent.
branches.path: Use the tutorial's Coding Agent tab: install required tooling and the render-deploy skill, then prompt the agent to deploy and monitor the service.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The route delegates configuration decisions to an agent and targets the same deployed-app terminal state.

branches.condition: The developer chooses a login method other than email and password.
branches.path: Use Google, GitHub, GitLab, or Bitbucket as the Render login provider; provider-specific account connection rules apply.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Authentication steps differ, but successful Dashboard access feeds into the same deployment route.

branches.condition: The app repository is hosted on GitLab or Bitbucket instead of GitHub.
branches.path: Add and authorize the matching Git deployment credential, then select the repository from that provider.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The provider authorization UI differs, but the service configuration and terminal deployed-app state remain the same.

branches.condition: The app consists only of static assets and no server-side code.
branches.path: Create a Static Site and provide its build and publish-directory settings instead of Web Service build and start settings.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The official tutorial ends at the same first-app-deployed milestone but the configuration and runtime requirements differ.

branches.condition: The developer is deploying a public repository they do not belong to.
branches.path: Select Public Git Repository, enter its URL, and connect it without Git-provider credentials.
branches.effect_on_first_success: The initial app can still be deployed, but auto-deploys and pull-request previews are not supported for this source type.

branches.condition: The application is in a monorepo.
branches.path: Specify the Root Directory that represents the application root before configuring build and start commands.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds one required configuration field for Render to locate and run the application correctly.

branches.condition: The app's language is not available as a native runtime or the repo is Docker-based.
branches.path: Select the Docker runtime and build from the repository's Dockerfile.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes runtime configuration while preserving the deployed-app terminal state.

branches.condition: The application requires environment variables or secrets.
branches.path: Add every required value before creating the service; if omitted, add it later and redeploy.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Missing values can add a failed-deploy and recovery cycle before the app becomes viewable.

branches.condition: The deploy status changes to Failed.
branches.path: Review the log feed, identify and fix the issue, then push a new commit to the linked branch so Render starts a new deploy.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds an unbounded diagnosis and redeploy loop before Live status and URL verification.

friction_gates.description: The tutorial requires a deployment-interface choice among Dashboard, CLI, and coding agent without declaring one preferred route.
A Render account is required before the developer can use Account Settings and create a service.
The developer must authorize Render's GitHub access and include the app repository.
The developer must classify the app as Web Service or Static Site based on whether it contains server-side code.
The linked deployment credential must have access to the repository selected for deployment.
The service creation flow requires a region choice, and the first-deploy docs do not prescribe one.
The selected runtime must match the application or its Dockerfile.
The developer must provide an application-specific build command.
The developer must provide an application-specific start command.
The developer must choose an instance type; Free avoids payment for this tutorial but has documented limitations.
The developer waits for Render to build, start, and evaluate the first deploy; no numeric duration is documented.
A Web Service must bind to a public HTTP port on 0.0.0.0 or the deploy fails.

time_to_first_success.value: in minutes; Render provides no numeric duration in the inspected first-deploy documentation

sources.title: Your First Render Deploy
sections_used: Run your web app in minutes
evidence_supported: Web Service versus Static Site rule

sources.title: Services and Service Types
sections_used: Which service type is right for my code?
evidence_supported: Web Service is the most common first service
Web Service is the public dynamic-app type

sources.title: Web Services
sections_used: Host dynamic web apps at a public URL
evidence_supported: Web Service public URL and port requirement

sources.title: Render account registration
evidence_supported: Direct unauthenticated response did not expose the registration form's fields or verification transitions without executing the application

uncertainties.question: Which deployment interface is Render's canonical first path: Dashboard, CLI, or coding agent?
uncertainties.impact: The route changes the installation, authentication, configuration, and ownership steps, so the knowledge base cannot claim one canonical complete journey without a human scope choice.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official first-deploy tutorial tells developers to select their preferred method and presents all three as peer tabs without naming one recommended method.

uncertainties.question: What exact fields and verification transitions are required during a new Render account registration?
uncertainties.impact: The account-creation gate cannot be decomposed into fully evidenced atomic substeps.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The tutorial only links Sign up for Render; login settings lists supported methods, while the unauthenticated registration page is client-rendered and did not expose form text in the inspected response.

uncertainties.question: What is the current Dashboard submission control label for the selected route?
uncertainties.impact: The action is reproducible as form submission, but the exact UI label is inconsistent across official docs.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: Your First Render Deploy says click Deploy, while Web Services says click Create Web Service.

excluded_after_success: Connecting Render Postgres or Key Value
Installing the Render CLI after a Dashboard deploy
Exploring private services, background workers, cron jobs, workflows, or other service types
Configuring production hardening or paid-instance features
